titleStmt.title: Memorandum by the Director of the Policy Planning Staff ( (Kennan))

div.head: Memorandum by the Director of the Policy Planning Staff (Kennan)Addressed to the Secretary of State and the Under Secretary of State (Lovett).
div.p: The National Security Council Staff, having taken note of the former Policy Planning Staff paper,No. PPS 39 of September 7, p. 146. has been trying to draft a rather elaborate statement of American objectives and policy in China.

This effort is, I believe, largely a result of the wide-spread feeling, which Secretary Forrestal appears to hold, that we “have no policy” with relation to what is now happening in China and that something should be done about it.

As the State Department Consultant for the NSC, I have declined to accept the draft produced by the NSC StaffSee NSC draft of November 2, p. 185. and have called for a meeting of the five Consultants to explain to them why I do not think it useful or advisable to try to produce such a policy document.

To support this position we have put down, in the form of a Staff paper,No. PPS 39/1, supra. certain of our views on this subject which I propose to submit
to the other Consultants, in advance of the meeting, in order that they may have a chance to consider our points of argument before the discussion.
Since I am not presenting this paper as an approved Department position, I am not asking for approval on it.
However, I thought it might be of interest to you and of possible usefulness in connection with questions that may be asked by the press in the coming period.
